outputs.accepted_verification_result_packet: Public-safe accepted result packet for scope ref scope:interface-smoke-public under policy: accept only rows with scoped result artifact and acceptance basis; blocked or inconclusive rows remain explicit. Upstream refs are read-only.
outputs.result_summary: Fixture contains 2 result artifact refs: 1 accepted pass row (VR-001) and 1 blocked/inconclusive row (VR-002). No broader owner acceptance is claimed beyond the provided public synthetic fixture policy.
outputs.accepted_result_rows: VR-001: verdict=pass; acceptance=accepted; basis=public synthetic smoke log ref; scope=scope:interface-smoke-public; status=accepted for downstream audit use within stated policy.
outputs.blocked_or_inconclusive_rows: VR-002: verdict=inconclusive; acceptance=blocked; basis=missing measurement artifact; blocker=measurement artifact absent, so row is not accepted and requires follow-up evidence or owner disposition.
outputs.acceptance_provenance: Accepted row VR-001 is based only on fixture result_artifact_refs entry and approved_result_acceptance_policy. Plan alignment ref is plan:interface-contract-v0. Scope ref is scope:interface-smoke-public.
outputs.boundary_review_note: Public-safe synthetic fixture only. No private project facts, credentials, customer names, production logs, unstated approvals, source authority replacement, or upstream artifact mutation are included or implied.

provenance: Workflow contract: accepted_verification_result_packet_v0 defines ownership of result_scope_identity, accepted_result_row_identity, acceptance_provenance, and result_summary.
Fixture approved_result_acceptance_policy explicitly states accepted rows require scoped result artifact and acceptance basis; blocked or inconclusive rows remain explicit.
Fixture result_artifact_refs explicitly provides VR-001 as pass/accepted with basis public synthetic smoke log ref.
Fixture result_artifact_refs explicitly provides VR-002 as inconclusive/blocked with basis missing measurement artifact.
gaps: Missing measurement artifact for VR-002 blocks acceptance of that row.
No trace_matrix_refs or owner_decision_refs were provided; no trace closure or owner approval beyond fixture policy is claimed.
No execution artifact contents were provided beyond public synthetic result refs; raw runtime truth is not asserted.
downstream_handoff: functional_configuration_audit_page_library_v0 may consume accepted_result_rows containing only VR-001 plus result_summary noting VR-002 is blocked/inconclusive.
review_gate_evidence_pack_v0 may consume the accepted result summary, blocker note for VR-002, and provenance list for later review.
Owner follow-up: provide scoped measurement artifact or explicit owner disposition for VR-002 before it can move from blocked/inconclusive to accepted.
boundary_review_note: Public boundary: all content is derived from the public-safe synthetic fixture and workflow contract text in the prompt.
Private/secret boundary: no private project data, credentials, customer identifiers, production logs, or undisclosed runtime details are present.
Upstream mutation boundary: verification plan refs, scope refs, and result artifact refs are treated as read-only references; this packet does not modify or replace them.
Acceptance boundary: acceptance is limited to VR-001 under the stated fixture policy; VR-002 remains blocked/inconclusive.
no_claims: Does not claim owner acceptance beyond the approved_result_acceptance_policy provided in the fixture.
Does not claim VR-002 passed or is accepted.
Does not claim trace matrix closure, FCA/SVR approval, production readiness, or source authority replacement.
Does not claim inspection of files, execution of tools, external verification, or access to private artifacts.
